news.title: Property registration values to go up from May first week in Telangana
news.para: The state government will revise property registration values from early May, ending a year-long deferral. The Hyderabad Industrial Land Transformation (HILT) Policy, allowing landowners to change land use for a fee, will also be implemented. These moves aim to boost resource mobilization and industrial transformation, with consultations planned for industrial associations.

news.title: Telangana fire department holds meeting with builders on proposed amendments to fire services act
news.para: Telangana Fire Department officials met with builders and developers to discuss proposed amendments to the Fire Services Act, 1999. The revisions aim to update regulations for modern urban infrastructure risks, enhance safety, and extend enforcement to buildings below 15 meters. This consultation is part of a process to strengthen fire safety and simplify compliance.

news.title: Kanpur development body board decides to reduce cost of 5,600 unsold flats
news.para: Kanpur Development Authority has decided to lower prices on 5,600 unsold flats. These flats will now be available at 2018 rates, offering significant discounts. The authority also passed a substantial budget for development projects in the current financial year. New infrastructure like a bus stand and a super-speciality hospital are planned.

news.title: EOW files case against four for selling assigned land to 80 buyers
news.para: Cyberabad Economic Offences Wing has filed a criminal case against four individuals. They are accused of defrauding 80 people by selling assigned land. The total amount collected from these buyers is Rs 3.48 crore. The accused allegedly created a fake layout and sold plots without legal rights. Police are currently questioning the accused.

news.title: Delhi: DDA still awaiting CBI clearance to start Signature View Apartments' demolition
news.para: Redevelopment of Signature View Apartments faces further delay. The Delhi Development Authority awaits Central Bureau of Investigation clearance to demolish the unsafe buildings. The CBI is investigating corruption allegations in the construction. Residents are eager for demolition to begin and return home. A structural audit recommended evacuation and dismantling. Manual demolition is planned due to safety concerns.

news.title: Haryana policy change drives developer interest in senior living
news.para: Haryana's new retirement housing policy is boosting senior living projects. Developers can now build more units, making projects more viable. This change encourages integrated communities with essential amenities. Demand is rising due to longer life spans and changing family structures. Major developers are entering this growing segment, which has significant untapped potential.

news.title: Delhi: MCD begins survey on misuse of residential premises following SC directions
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi has launched a city-wide survey to identify residential properties being used for non-residential purposes, following a Supreme Court directive. Zonal deputy commissioners are conducting time-bound field inspections to compile accurate lists of violations for an affidavit to be submitted to the apex court.

news.title: Revenue minister asks Mumbai Suburban collector to register FIR in ₹87-crore Borivali land row
news.para: Revenue Minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule has ordered an FIR against a builder and former government officers in the Rs 87 crore Borivali land compensation controversy. The Devrukhkar family alleges fraud and illegal disbursal of compensation for their ancestral property, which was reserved for a public garden.

news.title: Gurugram police arrest CKC Infra Company's director for defrauding investors
news.para: Gurugram police have arrested two executives of a construction firm tied to a fraudulent real estate scheme. The duo is under investigation for deceiving investors by promoting fictitious projects associated with Dhirendra Shastri's Aparna Ashram. They falsely secured investments through forged documents, luring investors with promises of high returns from properties in Gurugram and Noida.

news.title: Delhi court orders framing of charges against Three C Shelters' directors in Greenopolis housing project case
news.para: A Delhi court has initiated proceedings against the leaders of Three C Shelters Pvt Ltd, in relation to the halted Greenopolis housing project in Gurugram. The directors stand accused of deceiving homebuyers, with allegations pointing towards a preliminary case of fraud, forgery, and conspiracy.

news.title: Lodha Developers' net profit rises 24% in FY26
news.para: Lodha Developers' net profit grew by 24 percent to ₹3,430.7 crore for FY26. Total income also saw a significant increase of 20.82 percent. The board recommended a final dividend of ₹4.25 per share. New projects were added, boosting the gross development value to ₹2 lakh crore.

news.title: RBI imposes penalty on Bandhan Bank, Muthoot Housing Finance Company
news.para: The Reserve Bank of India has levied a Rs 41.8 lakh penalty on Bandhan Bank for failing to comply with norms, including Know Your Customer directions and periodic risk reviews. Additionally, Muthoot Housing Finance Company was fined Rs 80,000 for non-adherence to the Fair Practice Code. These actions stem from regulatory compliance deficiencies.

news.title: PropShare Celestia units end over 1% lower on trading debut
news.para: PropShare Celestia's trading debut saw its units open lower and close down 1.42% on Friday, listing at Rs 9,99,900.01 against an issue price of Rs 10,50,000. The SM REIT, aiming to acquire commercial space in Ahmedabad, had its Rs 244-crore IPO subscribed 1.33 times. The company's market valuation reached Rs 241.16 crore.

news.title: Chalet Hotels to acquire Udaipur resort for ₹171 crore
news.para: Chalet Hotels has approved the acquisition of Seasons Hotels, which owns a 144-room resort in Udaipur, for ₹171 crore. The deal marks the company’s entry into Rajasthan as it expands its leisure hospitality portfolio.

news.title: Unitech's nominee director Uma Shankar resigns
news.para: Realty firm Unitech Ltd announced that its nominee director, Uma Shankar, has resigned due to personal reasons, effective March 7, 2026. The company apologized for a delayed filing of this resignation, attributing it to an unintentional oversight. Shankar was appointed to the board in November 2022, following a Supreme Court directive for an independent management.

news.title: Odisha: 244 acres to be acquired for Jeypore Airport upgrade
news.para: The Odisha government is upgrading Jeypore Airport to Category 3C, requiring the acquisition of 244 acres. This expansion will allow the airport to handle 72-seater aircraft and operate 24/7, significantly boosting regional connectivity, economic growth, and tourism in southern Odisha.

news.title: Margao, Mormugao ODPs to be converted into masterplans
news.para: Goa's Town and Country Planning Department is set to transform existing development plans for Margao and Mormugao into advanced GIS-based masterplans. This initiative, part of the Centre's Amrut 2.0 program, will create digital blueprints for these cities. The process involves detailed data collection on various urban aspects. Experts anticipate the conversion will take over a year to complete.

news.title: Belagavi civic body's crackdown on illegal constructions raises accountability questions
news.para: Belagavi City Corporation intensified its drive against illegal constructions with a partial demolition of a four-storey building in Maruti Galli. The operation, while welcomed by some, has sparked controversy over alleged lack of notice and financial losses for tenants. Questions are also being raised about the corporation's monitoring and approval processes for such structures.

news.title: KHB plans a new residential layout on 92 acres in Belagavi
news.para: Karnataka Housing Board is planning a new residential project in Honnihal village. The scheme will be spread across 92 acres. This initiative aims to meet the growing housing needs of Belagavi city. Landowners will receive fair compensation and rehabilitation benefits. The government's approval is awaited. Public notification will follow the approval process.

news.title: Smart city mission is scrapped in Maharashtra, special purpose vehicles gone
news.para: Maharashtra government has dismantled Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) for the Smart City Mission across eight cities, including Nagpur. Municipal commissioners will now assume CEO roles and all SPV assets will be transferred to municipal corporations. This move signals the closure of the decade-long urban flagship program, with responsibilities shifting to local civic bodies.
